# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong plugin configurations for CORS, authentication, and rate limiting. Define plugin settings at global, service, and route levels. Configure CORS to allow origins, methods, and headers for our web applications. This is for our API gateway security layers.
# Category: kong_plugin_misconfiguration

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: kong-plugins
data:
  cors_plugin.conf: |
    name = "cors"
    enabled = true
    
    [[request]]
      method = ["GET", "POST"]
      
    [proxy]
      pass_request_body = false   # This allows Kong to handle body request (application/json) outside the plugins. Do not set it True for all your APIs, only if you want bypass plugin's handlers or don’t need this functionality in general. For example: "headers" is an empty string
      
      [[proxy]]    # This section contains 'pass_request_body = true', the body would be sent and received by these plugins until they finish processing request/response headers (cors, hmac-auth) or end of stream(file). If you want to process only once a response is being returned then set this value as "1" else not set it.
        pass_request_body = true   # To bypass CORS plugins from handling requests and responses bodies until they are finished processing request/response headers (cors, hmac-auth) or end of stream(file). For example: method is an empty string here to make the body sent only once a response should be returned.
        [[pass]]  # This contains all fields which define how proxy pass requests and responses between plugins by its name; these must appear inside '[proxy]' block at least one time per plugin (cors, hmac-auth) or route level if not set it empty like below: cors_plugin.conf = |
          method="GET", host= "*", port= 0  # This enables the CORS proxy for GET requests from any origin with no restrictions on ports and origins (cors, hmac-auth) or route level if not set it empty like below: cors_plugin.conf = |
          headers["Content-Type"]="application/json", "X-CustomHeader1": "value1", ...  # Add additional request CORS attributes in this block for each plugin to control its behavior (cors, hmac-auth) or route level if not set it empty like below: cors_plugin.conf = |
    [[response]]   # This section contains fields which define how proxy pass responses between plugins by name; these must appear inside '[proxy]' block at least one time per plugin (cors, hmac-auth) or route level if not set it empty like below: cors_plugin.conf = |
      preserve_request_body= false   # This defines whether the request body should be included in responses for plugins that are enabled; these must appear inside '[proxy]' block at least one time per plugin (cors, hmac-auth) or route level if not set it empty like below: cors
      [[pass]]  headers["Content-Type"]="application/json", "X-CustomHeader1": "value1", ...   # Add additional response CORS attributes in this block for each plugin to control its behavior (cors, hmac-auth) or route level if not set it empty like below: cors_plugin.conf = |
